info.title: Mintlify Static Export API
info.description: Programmatically generate a self-contained static export of your documentation and download it as a single bundle. Available on Enterprise plans.

post.description:
Start a static export job for a deployment. The job pre-renders your documentation into a self-contained set of static HTML, RSC, and asset files. Returns a job ID you can use to poll status and, once complete, generate a downloadable bundle.

Static export is available on Enterprise plans.

Authenticate with an admin API key.

202.description: The export job was accepted and queued.

400.description: The request body is invalid. Check that `domain` is a reachable hostname and that `paths` entries are valid page paths.

403.description: Static export is not enabled for this organization. Contact sales to enable it on an Enterprise plan.

get.description:
Retrieve the current status and progress of a static export job. Poll this endpoint after starting a job until `status` is `completed` (or `failed`).

Static export is available on Enterprise plans.

Authenticate with an admin API key.

parameters.name: jobId
parameters.description: The ID of the static export job returned by `Start static export job`.

200.description: The current state of the export job.

404.description: No job exists with the provided ID.

post.description:
Package a completed static export job into a single archive and return a download link. The link is a presigned S3 URL — download it before `expiresAt`.

The job must have a `status` of `completed` before a bundle can be generated.

Static export is available on Enterprise plans.

Authenticate with an admin API key.

parameters.name: jobId
parameters.description: The ID of a completed static export job.

200.description: A presigned S3 link to the static export bundle.

404.description: No job exists with the provided ID.

409.description: The job has not finished yet, so a bundle cannot be generated.

bearerAuth.description: The Authorization header expects a Bearer token. Use an admin API key. This is a server-side secret key. Generate one on the [API keys page](https://app.mintlify.com/settings/organization/api-keys) in your dashboard.

domain.description: The primary domain of the deployment to export. Use the custom domain configured for your project in the Mintlify dashboard (for example, `docs.example.com`). If you haven't configured a custom domain, use your Mintlify subdomain (for example, `acme.mintlify.app`). Provide the hostname only. Do not include the protocol, a trailing slash, or a path prefix such as `/docs`.

version.description: An optional version label to tag this export. Defaults to the latest published version.

paths.description: An optional list of page paths to include. When omitted, every published page is exported.

jobId.description: Unique identifier for the static export job.

status.description: The current state of the job.

pageCount.description: The number of pages exported so far.

updatedAt.description: When the job was last updated.

error.description: A human-readable error message. Present only when `status` is `failed`.

jobId.description: The ID of the job this bundle was generated for.

bundleUrl.description: A presigned S3 link to the static export bundle archive. Download it before the link expires.

sizeBytes.description: The size of the bundle in bytes.

error.description: A human-readable description of the error.
